steps.title: Isolate the variable term
steps.content: Starting from $$7(2x-3)=63,$$ divide both sides by 7 to isolate the variable term.

steps.title: Identify the answer
steps.content: This gives $$2x-3=9.$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. Dividing each side of the given equation by $7$ yields $\frac{7\left(2x-3\right)}{7}=\frac{63}{7}$, or $2x-3=9$. Therefore, the equation $2x-3=9$ is equivalent to the given equation and has the same solution.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This equation is equivalent to $7\left(2x-3\right)=392$, not $7\left(2x-3\right)=63$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C is incorrect. Distributing $7$ on the left-hand side of the given equation yields $14x-21=63$, not $2x-21=63$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. Distributing $7$ on the left-hand side of the given equation yields $14x-21=63$, not $2x-21=70$.
